description_long: This barn features a tall, steeply pitched roof with a simple, symmetrical form, giving it a classic rural silhouette. The roof is a gable type, sloping down on two sides, and extends over the main structure, providing ample coverage. The exterior siding is composed of evenly spaced horizontal planks, creating a clean and orderly appearance. There are no visible doors or windows on any side, suggesting a utilitarian design focused on storage or shelter. The muted brown color and subtle surface markings evoke the look of weathered wood. Its overall form and proportions are reminiscent of traditional American barns commonly found in agricultural settings.
description: A tall, brown barn with a steep gable roof and horizontal plank siding, lacking visible doors or windows. Its simple, symmetrical design is typical of classic American rural barns.
description_view_0: The barn is seen from the broad side, showing the full width of the gable roof and the horizontal plank siding below.
description_view_1: This view highlights the narrow gable end, emphasizing the steep pitch of the roof and the triangular profile.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this side displays the wide face of the barn with the expansive roof and horizontal siding.
description_view_3: Another gable end view, mirroring view 1, with a sharp triangular roof and no visible openings.
attribute_door_style: single large hinged door (implied, not visible)
